channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Honda Elevate Facelift To Borrow City's Tricks: Still A Wait
item.description: Honda confirms an Elevate facelift with 360-camera, ventilated seats and the City's new 10.25-inch screen, due in H2 2026. Our verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV Teased For June 30 Debut: Harrier EV Hardware In A Cult Skin
item.description: Tata Motors teases the Sierra EV ahead of its June 30 debut. Expect Harrier EV battery and motor hardware in the cult nameplate's body.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JLR Backtracks: Range Rover, Defender, Discovery Get Hybrids; Jaguar Stays EV-Only
item.description: JLR will offer mild-hybrid, full-hybrid, PHEV and EV options across Range Rover, Defender and Discovery. Jaguar stays EV-only. Our take on the U-turn.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton Teased As 'Baby Patrol': July 9 Mumbai Debut Set
item.description: Nissan teases the Tekton midsize SUV as a 'Baby Patrol' ahead of its July 9 Mumbai global debut. The Car Jury weighs what it means for Creta buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Ebella Deliveries Begin: A Pricier eVitara With a Toyota Badge
item.description: Toyota Ebella deliveries start with the top-spec E3 at Rs 23.60 lakh. BaaS option drops the entry to Rs 15.25 lakh. Our verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Porsche Borrows Hyundai's Homework: 2027 Taycan Gets Fake Gears
item.description: The 2027 Porsche Taycan gets E-Shift simulated gearshifts inspired by the Hyundai Ioniq 5 N, plus a Manthey kit and new infotainment. TCJ's verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia hikes prices by up to 2 percent from July 1: buy in June if you're ready
item.description: Kia India will raise prices across its range by up to 2 percent, or as much as Rs 2.6 lakh, from July 1, 2026. Here's what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Force Motors Hits 200,000 Mercedes Engines: Why This Chakan Milestone Matters
item.description: Force Motors rolls out its 200,000th Mercedes-Benz engine at Chakan, a 6-cyl M256 petrol for the GLS 450. Here is what the milestone really signals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Bengaluru Now Runs The Brain Of Your Next Mercedes
item.description: Mercedes CTO Jorg Burzer says Bengaluru is now central to software, infotainment and autonomous driving work for the three-pointed star.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BMW's profit warning is a warning shot for the entire German luxury trio in India
item.description: BMW shares crash to 2020 lows after a China-and-Iran profit warning drags Mercedes and Volkswagen with it. Here is what it means for Indian buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: India-UK Trade Pact Opens Door to 3.78 Lakh British Cars at Slashed Duties
item.description: India will let in 3.78 lakh UK-made cars at duties as low as 10 percent over 15 years under CETA. Here's what it means for Indian buyers and makers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Innova Crysta Facelift Lands At Dealers: Same Old Money-Spinner, Mildly Refreshed
item.description: Toyota Innova Crysta facelift starts arriving at dealers, with GX+ from Rs 21.15 lakh and ZX at Rs 26.63 lakh ex-showroom. Our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai's Sub-4m Electric SUV Spied: Diwali 2026 Punch EV Killer Incoming
item.description: Hyundai's sub-4m electric SUV, codenamed HE1i, has been spied testing in South Korea ahead of a likely 2026 Diwali launch in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai's i20 N Shadow Edition farewell is a reminder of what India never got
item.description: Hyundai is sending off the i20 N with a 100-unit Shadow Edition for Australia. India never got the hot hatch, and that is the real story.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Citroen eC3X at Rs 10.25 Lakh: Cheaper, Better Kitted, Still a Hard Sell
item.description: Citroen has launched the eC3X at Rs 10.25 lakh with 6 airbags, LED lights and a BaaS scheme. The Car Jury's verdict on whether it's enough.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mini Countryman C at Rs 47.5 lakh: local assembly finally makes the badge sensible
item.description: Mini launches the locally assembled Countryman C in India at Rs 47.5 lakh, pitching it against the BMW X1, Mercedes GLA and Audi Q3.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Wagon R Bioflex Reaches Private Buyers: Symbolism, Not a Sensible Buy Yet
item.description: Maruti opens Wagon R Bioflex sales to private buyers at Rs 7.24 lakh ex-showroom. India's first flex-fuel production car, but is it worth buying?
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Maruti Brezza Facelift Spotted Production-Ready: Launch Imminent
item.description: Maruti's 2026 Brezza facelift has been spied in production-ready guise during a TVC shoot. New grille, bumper and a 6-speed manual are confirmed.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BMW iX5 to Pack a 144kWh Battery: India Should Wait for the X5 Diesel
item.description: BMW's upcoming iX5 will get a 141-144kWh battery, the brand's largest yet. The Car Jury on what it means for X5 buyers in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XEV 9S Pack One Above Deliveries Start: The Smart Pick at Rs 19.95 Lakh
item.description: Mahindra begins XEV 9S Pack One Above deliveries at Rs 19.95 lakh ex-showroom, undercutting the XEV 9e while adding kit. Our verdict for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Audi A6 allroad returns with PHEV: a global wagon we still won't get
item.description: Audi reveals the fifth-gen A6 allroad with a 367hp plug-in hybrid and a 299hp V6 diesel. The Car Jury on why India buyers shouldn't wait for it.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Finally Admits Touch-Only Cabins Were a Mistake
item.description: Tata Motors will reintroduce physical controls alongside touchscreens after customer pushback, says design chief Martin Uhlarik. The Car Jury's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Owns the 4.5-4.8m SUV Segment: May 2026 Sales Decoded
item.description: Mahindra took over three-fourths of the 4.5-4.8m SUV segment in May 2026, with Scorpio and XUV7XO leading. The Car Jury's verdict on what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Wagon R Bioflex at Rs 7.24 lakh: flex fuel, fleet-only, and a tough sell
item.description: Maruti's Wagon R Bioflex flex-fuel hatch is priced at Rs 7.24 lakh ex-showroom, costs Rs 86,000 over the ZXi+ petrol, and is fleet-only.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: India Legalises E100 Ethanol Cars: Symbolic Win, Buyer Cost Still Unclear
item.description: Nitin Gadkari has signed rules legalising 100% ethanol vehicles in India. Toyota, Hyundai, Mahindra and MG are expected to launch E100 models soon.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Teases B-Segment EV Hatch for Europe: India Should Watch Closely
item.description: MG teases a B-segment electric hatchback for Europe at Goodwood 2026, taking on the VW ID. Polo, Renault 5 and Nissan Micra from 2027.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Elevate ZX gets Rs 2.15 lakh off in June: dealer panic, not generosity
item.description: Honda is offering up to Rs 2.15 lakh off the Elevate ZX in June 2026, plus benefits on the Amaze and pre-facelift City. Here is what it really means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan's India Reboot: Five New Cars, And The Tekton Is The One That Matters
item.description: Nissan is rolling out five new and updated models for India, led by the Duster-based Tekton SUV launching in July. Here is what is coming.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: E100 Gets The Green Light: Toyota Is Ready, The Pumps Are Not
item.description: Nitin Gadkari signs E100 fuel regulations, clearing flex-fuel cars from Toyota, Hyundai, MG and Suzuki. Why infrastructure, not approval, is the bottleneck.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2027 Kia Sonet And New Creta Caught Testing Together In Manali
item.description: Test mules of the 2027 Kia Sonet and next-gen Hyundai Creta were spotted together in Manali, hinting at a shared development push.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Grand Vitara, Invicto Get Up To Rs 2.15 Lakh Off In June 2026
item.description: Maruti is offering up to Rs 2.15 lakh in benefits on the Invicto and Grand Vitara this June 2026, along with discounts on Nexa hatchbacks.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: India Frees Up Radar Spectrum: ADAS Just Got Cheaper for Every Carmaker
item.description: India scraps licensing for 77-81GHz automotive radar and 5.9GHz V2X bands, aligning with the US and EU and clearing the runway for cheaper ADAS.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata's Second 2026 Price Hike: Buy Before July 1 If You're Already Decided
item.description: Tata Motors will raise prices of its ICE and EV cars by up to 1.5 percent, or as much as Rs 43,485, from July 1, 2026. Here's our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda's May 2026 Slide: Kylaq Down 30%, Kushaq Doubles, Portfolio Wobbles
item.description: Skoda India sold 5,760 units in May 2026, down 14.5% YoY. Kylaq slid 30%, Kushaq doubled. The Car Jury's take on what is going wrong.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XUV7XO AX Deliveries Begin: The Sensible Pick At Rs 13.66 Lakh
item.description: Mahindra begins XUV7XO base AX variant deliveries at Rs 13.66 lakh petrol and Rs 14.96 lakh diesel, with bi-LED headlamps standard.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Design is the new horsepower: why Mahindra and Tata are winning the showroom
item.description: ETAuto argues design is now the lead driver in India's car market. The Car Jury's verdict on what Mahindra, Tata and Hyundai are really selling.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: EV Wait Times Hit Three Months as Fuel Panic Rewrites India's Buying Math
item.description: Fuel prices spiked by the Iran war have pushed Indian EV demand to record highs, with Maruti, Mercedes-Benz and BMW deliveries stretching to three months.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai i20 Lands In Brazil At Rs 18.77 Lakh: India Misses The Bigger, Bolder Hatch
item.description: Hyundai launches the 4th-gen i20 in Brazil at BRL 99,000 (Rs 18.77 lakh) on the K3 platform with ADAS, sitting between the HB20 and Creta.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda's E-Clutch Hits the Hornet and Transalp, but the Price Hike Stings
item.description: Honda has launched E-Clutch versions of the CB750 Hornet and XL750 Transalp in India, with prices up over Rs 1 lakh. Our verdict on whether it is worth it.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Citroen piles Rs 1.4 lakh on Aircross and Basalt: discounts won't fix the problem
item.description: Citroen is offering up to Rs 1.4 lakh off on the Aircross, Basalt and C3 through June 2026. Our verdict on whether the discount is enough.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Next-gen Hyundai i20 borrows Venue's twin screens, and that's the right call
item.description: Hyundai teases the fourth-gen i20 cabin with twin 12.3-inch screens lifted from the Venue. Debut expected during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Duster's Cheaper Automatic Is The Variant That Should Have Launched On Day One
item.description: Renault plans a more affordable automatic variant of the new Duster after 5,000 sales. Here's why TCJ thinks this trim was overdue from launch.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Kylaq Sportline Locks September 2026 Launch, 1.5 TSI Still On The Table
item.description: Skoda confirms Kylaq Sportline for September 2026 with sportier styling. A 1.5 TSI Kylaq RS is under evaluation but not yet greenlit for India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Government Exempts E22 to E30 Ethanol Blends From Excise Duty: A Solution Without Cars
item.description: The Centre has exempted E22, E25, E27 and E30 ethanol-blended petrol from excise duty, but none of these fuels is on sale yet in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV Debuts June 30: AWD On The Table, 500+ KM Range On The Cards
item.description: Tata Sierra EV debuts June 30 with an expected 500+ km range and a likely AWD option. Here is what it means for the Creta EV and BE6 fight.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2027 Hyundai i20 Leaks: Crossover Styling, But India Isn't On The Guest List
item.description: The next-gen Hyundai i20 has leaked with rugged crossover styling and a new cabin ahead of its global debut. Here is what it means for India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault confirms cheaper Duster automatics: about time, and still not enough
item.description: Renault Group India CEO Stephane Deblaise confirms more affordable Duster automatic variants are coming to plug the gap above the 1.0 manual range.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BMW F 450 GS Accessory List: Cross-Spoke Wheels Cost Nearly Rs 1 Lakh
item.description: BMW F 450 GS accessory prices revealed in India. Cross-spoke wheels cost Rs 99,693, Rallye windshield starts at Rs 2,000. Full list and our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Kylaq Sportline Lands September 2026: Cosmetic Quick-Fix, Not the RS Hot-Hatch SUV You Wanted
item.description: Skoda confirms a Sportline trim for the Kylaq in September 2026. A 1.5 TSI is being considered but a full RS version is unlikely.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV Debuts June 30: The Harrier EV's Smaller, Smarter Sibling
item.description: Tata Motors will debut the Sierra EV on June 30, 2026, slotting between Curvv EV and Harrier EV with RWD and AWD options on acti.ev+.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
